commit.message:
Standardize all views to header/body/footer layout pattern

- Every module view now follows: Header → Divider → Body → Footer
- Footer always pinned at bottom with consistent actions per state:
  * Empty state: ScanButton (primary) in footerBar
  * Scanning: ghost Stop button in footerBar
  * Results: actionBar with label + Rescan ghost + primary action
  * Success: ghost Rescan button in footerBar
- EmptyStateView/SuccessStateView are now purely informational (no buttons)
- Added ghostButton() and footerBar() shared helpers in ViewHelpers
- All inline Stop/Rescan buttons moved to standardized footer
- Added Rescan to UpdateView and DeclutterView (all tabs)
- Extracted resultsList/threatsBody/updatesList for cleaner view bodies

Views updated: SmartCareView, CleanView, ProtectView, UpdateView,
SpeedView, SpaceLensView, UninstallView, DeclutterView

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(theme): fix light mode by removing @media prefers-color-scheme overrides

Root cause: @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) blocks in CSS overrode
the class-based .dark toggle, making light mode impossible on dark-OS
systems. Deleted ~190 lines of redundant @media dark blocks from
design-tokens.css, globals.css, and admin design-tokens.css.

Additional fixes:
- tailwind.config.js: darkMode ['class','media'] → 'class' (fixes 764
  broken dark: utility classes)
- layout.tsx: inline script now handles 'system' localStorage value
  (prevents FOUC)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial release: nesstar-converter v1.0.0

Reverse-engineered Nesstar binary format converter with 100% cell-level
accuracy validated against official Nesstar Explorer text exports.

Features:
- 8 output formats: CSV, TSV, Parquet, Excel, Stata, JSON, JSONL, FWF
- Built-in validation against Nesstar Explorer exports
- Batch conversion of multi-block Nesstar files
- User-friendly CLI with info, convert, validate, batch, formats commands
- DDI XML metadata auto-detection
- Memory-mapped I/O for large files
- 45 unit tests, CI for Python 3.10-3.13

Validated against:
- EUS 1983 (38th Round): 9/9 blocks, 3.4M rows, zero mismatches
- HCES 1983, 1989-90, 2009-10: 27/28 blocks, 23.4M rows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: offline mode enforces no API fallback in cache.get() + fix arg forwarding in research_local.sh

Code review findings:
- HIGH: research_local.sh never forwarded --days/--pairs to sync step
- MEDIUM: cache.get() silently fell back to live API in offline mode

Fixes:
- cache.get(offline=True) raises RuntimeError on miss/empty instead of API call
- pipeline.py uses functools.partial to bind offline=True when in offline mode
- research_local.sh now forwards --days/--pairs/--quality-pairs to both sync and research
- Added 2 new tests for offline RuntimeError behavior (689 total passing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: rewrite README as step-by-step porting narrative

Transform the dry technical reference into a blog-post-style porting diary
that walks through each stage of getting Pokémon Red running in a browser:
ROM assembly, emulator selection, staging/patching, Emscripten compilation,
browser shell orchestration, and static deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: Learn Docs M365 Copilot agent

Declarative agent for M365 Copilot Chat backed by the Microsoft Learn
MCP Server (https://learn.microsoft.com/api/mcp). Tool-only, no backend.

- 3 tools: microsoft_docs_search, microsoft_code_sample_search, microsoft_docs_fetch

- Plugin manifest v2.4 with response_semantics for citation links
- Agent instructions for search-first documentation grounding

commit.message:
fix(benchmarks): enforce Security/Governance benchmarks in build profile (#4634)

* Initial plan

* fix(benchmarks): add bench_policy_evaluation target, fix security suite gate, add CI workflow

- Add explicit add_executable(bench_policy_evaluation) to benchmarks/CMakeLists.txt.
  The file only uses a mock PolicyEngine (no real deps), so it only links
  ${BENCHMARK_LIBS} + Threads::Threads.

- Fix cmake/SuiteGating.cmake: security suite group was gated behind enterprise
  features (THEMIS_ENABLE_HSM_REAL, THEMIS_ENABLE_S3, etc.) or ENTERPRISE edition.
  Core security benchmarks (AES-GCM, RBAC, policy evaluation, audit log) rely
  only on OpenSSL + themis_core, so the gate is now unconditional (set TRUE).
  This ensures bench_security, bench_policy_evaluation,
  bench_governance_policy_latency, and bench_compliance_security_governance are
  included in themis_benchmarks_all_eligible in every build configuration.

- Update tools/perf_expectations_audit.py check #3 to also verify
  bench_policy_evaluation source + CMake target.

- Add .github/workflows/02-feature-modules_security_security-governance-benchmark-ci.yml:
  dedicated CI job that builds all four security/governance benchmark targets and
  smoke-runs them (--benchmark_min_time=0.1s). Triggers on PR/push touching
  security/governance sources, benchmarks/CMakeLists.txt, or SuiteGating.cmake.

commit.message:
fix: fault-tolerant TZIP-21 metadata deserialization

Rewrite TokenMetadata.UnmarshalJSON to decode via map[string]json.RawMessage
so no single malformed field can crash the entire wallet sync. Every string
field is coerced through rawJSONToString, the formats array is decoded
entry-by-entry with malformed entries skipped, and non-object metadata
(string, array, number) is handled gracefully instead of returning an error.

This fixes sync failures caused by on-chain metadata with non-string types
in fields like mimeType, description, and name — which is valid on Tezos
since token metadata has no enforced schema.

Adds 10 new test cases covering format field resilience (array mimeType,
non-array formats, malformed entries, non-object metadata blobs) and 6
new fuzz seeds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(infra): upgrade worker OpenAI role to Contributor for Batch API

The worker container's managed identity had 'Cognitive Services OpenAI User'
which lacks the files/write data action required by the Batch API file upload
endpoint (POST /openai/files).

Upgrade to 'Cognitive Services OpenAI Contributor' (a001fd3d-...) which grants
Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/OpenAI/* — covering files/write and
batches/* needed for Global Batch processing.

This restores Batch API functionality and the 50% cost savings over individual
API calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: redirect embedded static asset paths to root

Cached pages with old relative asset references cause browsers to
resolve _content/ and _framework/ paths against the page URL, producing
URLs like /movie/.../discs/_content/Foo/bar.js. These match greedy
Razor route templates and cause 404s with wasted DB queries.

The LowercaseUrlMiddleware now detects /_content/ and /_framework/
appearing at non-root positions and 301-redirects to the correct
root-relative path (e.g. /_content/Foo/bar.js).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Discovers symbols from cache filenames in offline mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix TTL entry merges to use max() instead of last-writer-wins

Add merge_ttl_current() helper to LedgerDelta that takes
max(live_until_ledger_seq) for TTL entries during Updated+Updated
coalescing, while preserving last-writer-wins for all other entry types.

This fixes two consensus-critical bugs:
- Cross-cluster: delta.merge() Updated+Updated for TTL keys now takes max
- Intra-cluster: record_update() Updated→Updated for TTL keys now takes max

Matches stellar-core's maybeMergeRoTTLBumps semantics. TTL key with
non-TTL data is treated as a hard error (invariant violation).

Includes 10 regression tests covering both orderings, non-TTL unchanged
behavior, Created+Updated not max-merged, and pipeline-level scenarios
for intra-cluster, cross-cluster, and RW-after-RO cases.

Closes #1512

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 17: 10 fixes from real Dentsu scenario learnings

1. Remove dead DSP_BUYERS comments + duplicate Market Config header
2. Fix Xandr Invest member_id — user-entered IDs now flow to deal creation
3. Add PUBLISHER_FORMAT_AVAIL matrix (Outlook Video=❌, MCG Native=❌)
4. Fix deal naming: Microsoft_{market}_O&O_{format}_PMP_{name}_{DSP}
5. Rate card floor guard — warns when CPM < floor, links to Power BI report
6. Persist DSP + seat code across queue items (no re-entry for batch deals)
7. Deal-level frequency cap support (frequency_cap + frequency_cap_type)
8. Publisher×format validation in UI + validate_queue_item() blocks invalid combos
9. DV360 default — already first in DSP_REGISTRY (confirmed)
10. Bulk template — added multi-buyer tip caption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: Add cmd entrypoint and fix .gitignore to ignore only root binary\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace tree-sitter-dart git URL with tarball to fix npm install crash (#811)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace tree-sitter-dart git URL with tarball URL to fix npm install crash

The `github:` git URL for tree-sitter-dart caused npm's arborist to
create a dependency node with a null target during the rebuild phase,
crashing global installs with:
  Cannot destructure property 'package' of 'node.target' as it is null.

Using a GitHub archive tarball URL instead avoids the arborist bug while
still installing from the exact same commit (80e23c0).

Fixes #805

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus/sessions/c15109ae-0865-4d69-bd08-9972dcfe18f9

commit.message:
fix: remove ga4 secrets from history, add to gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(smoketest): add Backstage cluster registration and connectivity steps

- simulate-pipeline.sh: add 'Backstage Integration' stage after Validation
  - 'Print Backstage Registration Command' step: prints exact curl commands
  - 'Register cluster in Backstage catalog' step: gets Keycloak M2M token,
    POSTs to /api/cluster-registration/register, verifies 201/200 response
  - 'Verify Backstage AKS connectivity' step: logs in as Backstage SP,
    runs kubectl cluster-info to confirm the SP can reach the AKS API server,
    then restores ARM credentials for the cleanup trap
- cleanup trap: deregister cluster from Backstage (DELETE endpoint) before
  terraform destroy — best-effort, non-blocking
- .env.smoketest.template: add BACKSTAGE_URL, BACKSTAGE_KC_CLIENT_SECRET,
  BACKSTAGE_SP_CLIENT_ID, BACKSTAGE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET with comments
- react-ts-app/template/catalog-info.yaml: replace deployment-target-cluster
  with backstage.io/kubernetes-cluster annotation so the Backstage Kubernetes
  plugin can associate the component with its target cluster

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update navigation to mega-nav dropdown on all non-index pages

Replace old flat nav (nav-toggle/nav-links), legal nav (ppf-legal-nav),
path nav (path-nav), and local nav (local-nav) with the new mega-nav
hamburger dropdown navigation on all pages:

- Root pages: memberships, proof, start, contact, privacy-policy, terms-of-service
- Path pages: paths/index, paths/athlete, paths/adult, paths/integrated
- Local pages: all 5 local/*.html files

Changes per file:
- Replace old nav HTML with site-nav mega-nav dropdown
- Add ppf-mega-nav.css and ppf-homepage-redesign.css to <head>
- Add ppf-mega-nav.js script
- Add style.css where missing (legal/local pages)
- Remove section-indicator dots from proof.html
- Adjust relative paths for subdirectory pages (paths/, local/)

Also includes pre-existing index.html mega-nav update and js/main.js
cursor selector update for new nav classes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Jamalcamp30 <270403360+Jamalcamp30@users.noreply.github.com>
